hero.subtitle: One incredible week of hands-on kosher cooking, baking, beach-town adventures, Jewish pride, and the best summer energy for kids ages 3–12.

photos.headline: Camp photos
photos.body: Once camp begins, this section will feature fresh moments from the kitchen, beach-town trips, activities, and all the smiles in between.
photos.callout_body: During camp, new albums can be added for each day, trip, or special activity.

faq.question: What ages is camp for?
faq.answer: Camp welcomes kids ages 3–12. Activities and cooking stations are adapted by age so every camper can fully participate and succeed.

faq.question: What are drop-off and pickup times?
faq.answer: Drop-off is at 9:00 AM. Pickup is at 3:00 PM. Optional Before & After Care extends the day from 8:30 AM to 3:30 PM.

faq.question: How does drop-off and pickup work?
faq.answer: When you arrive, a staff member will greet your child. At pickup, only individuals listed on your registration form are authorized to collect your camper — please have ID ready.

faq.question: Why only one week?
faq.answer: One week is exactly enough to make it magical. We pack everything — cooking, trips, Jewish pride, friendships — into five unforgettable days. It's exclusive by design.

faq.question: Will there be security on site?
faq.answer: Yes. Camp will have security presence during camp hours, along with staff-supervised drop-off, pickup, and activities.

faq.question: What should my child bring?
faq.answer: Comfortable clothes they don't mind getting messy (aprons are provided), a water bottle, sunscreen, any allergy medications, and a great appetite. We take care of everything else.

faq.question: My child has food allergies — is camp safe?
faq.answer: We take allergies seriously. Note all allergies during registration. Our staff reviews every camper's needs. For severe allergies, we'll speak with you directly before camp begins.

faq.question: Are scholarships available?
faq.answer: We believe every Jewish child deserves this experience. Limited scholarship assistance is available. Contact rabbi@jewishpedro.org before registering to apply.
